resource_budget_record.value_hypothesis: A serving-memory optimization is useful only when aggregate throughput, KV-cache memory, and single-request verification remain separate budget dimensions.
resource_budget_record.capacity_pool: serving memory accounting fixture for KV-cache and batching records
cost_estimate: KV-cache memory budget recorded separately from token cost
PagedAttention-style serving memory page pressure recorded as capacity, not quality
batching and aggregate throughput are accounting dimensions only
verification_tax: single-request verified output check remains separate
displaced_costs: batching and aggregate throughput can hide tail latency and single-request risk
resource_budget_record.quality_predicate: Aggregate throughput cannot substitute for single-request verified output.
safety_gates: single-request verified output remains separate from aggregate throughput
resource_budget_record.escalation_rule: Do not dispatch or promote serving-memory savings until single-request verified-output and residual accounting are recorded separately.
residuals: KV-cache and batching figures are residual serving-memory accounting until measured in a real serving run.
Single-request verified output and model quality are not inferred from aggregate throughput.

non_claims: Does not promote any support state.
Does not prove scheduler behavior, runtime behavior, load stability, KV-cache behavior, serving throughput, single-request quality, or economic outcomes.
